evidence_quotes.quote: Dark patterns like fake "Only 5 minutes until this deal is gone for good!" offers on a site that never removes said offer
misleading marketing by prominent figures in the digital space
stopping videogame publishers from disabling and/or otherwise making their games, live service or otherwise, inaccessible to the people who own them and paid for them

paragraphs.text: Greetings! After reading through the details of The Digital Fairness Act, I can't help but feel this would be a wonderful proposal to support. Dark patterns like fake "Only 5 minutes until this deal is gone for good!" offers on a site that never removes said offer, addictive or predatory designs, malicious and heavy personalisation and misleading marketing by prominent figures in the digital space are all issues that plague said digital space heavily, and should definitely be rectified. Additionally, fitting in with the concept of the Digital Fairness Act and adding onto it, as many others already have, I'd like to put forth the option of adding the goal of stopping videogame publishers from disabling and/or otherwise making their games, live service or otherwise, inaccessible to the people who own them and paid for them, at a later arbitrary point in time, effectively rendering them completely destroyed and lost. The digital space is rife with such wonderful, interactive experiences, new and old, fully lost to time, hundreds of them fully missing. This problem isn't just one of the past either, to prove this is still an undergoing and very large issue, many of the most prominent live-service (and even non-live-service) games being played today are at risk of being inaccessible to anyone who bought or owns or otherwise should have access to these games, at one point in the future when the publisher decides to permanently disable them, due to a missing End-Of-Life plan that would make sure the customers that paid for their games would still have access to a playable state once the game company ends support. This feels extremely in line with the current sentiments already present in the Digital Fairness Act, and would help preserve hundreds if not thousands of games in the future from being lost media. Videogames have long since changed from a mere pastime, to true and genuine interactive artistic pieces, as millions and millions of developers put their heart and soul into their works, just for them to be inaccessible 10 or 20 years later due to the game company they worked for ceasing support. I believe it's completely unacceptable to withhold and even destroy such precious interactive experiences, as a foundational principle, no matter what the circumstance. Such an issue needs to be rectified, alongside all the other issues listed in the proposal. The feeling of being unable to even experience a fraction of the joy I had as a child when playing one of my favourite games as a child, because said game has been de-listed, or is completely unplayable, or the servers have shut down years ago with no community-driven option, knowing that no other person will ever be able to experience the joy I did, knowing that tens of thousands of developer, artist, managerial and promotional hours went to complete and utter waste, knowing that no matter how hard I or others may try, nothing will capture what that game really was and meant for me, as well as what it meant for hundreds of thousands or millions of other fans, that feeling is a feeling I do not want anyone else on this planet to experience. And so, I would highly implore all of us to do something about it.
